summary: The TokenVault contract demonstrates a strong security posture for a single-asset ERC-20 vault. It correctly applies ReentrancyGuard on all state-changing external functions, uses OpenZeppelin's SafeERC20 for transfer safety, and follows the checks-effects-interactions pattern in withdraw. The two identified findings are low-severity design observations rather than exploitable vulnerabilities.
mantle_specific: No cross-layer (L1↔L2) risks detected. The contract does not interact with Mantle bridge contracts or make assumptions about L1 finality. Gas costs are consistent with Mantle Sepolia's reduced fee model; no unbounded loops or excessive storage operations found.

findings.title: Owner is a single immutable EOA with no transfer mechanism
findings.description: The owner is set to msg.sender at construction and stored as an immutable address. There is no ownership transfer path.
findings.impact: If the deployer wallet is compromised or lost, ownership cannot be transferred. The recoverForeignToken admin function becomes permanently inaccessible.
findings.recommendation: Replace the immutable owner pattern with OpenZeppelin Ownable2Step to allow safe two-step ownership transfers.

findings.title: Fee-on-transfer tokens revert silently with UnsupportedTokenBehavior
findings.description: The received != amount guard correctly rejects fee-on-transfer tokens, but the revert reason may be unclear to integrators who do not read the source.
findings.impact: A misconfigured deployment with a fee token will fail at runtime rather than at deploy time. Integrators may not understand the root cause.
findings.recommendation: Add a constructor-level check verifying the asset is not a fee-on-transfer token, or document the limitation explicitly in NatSpec.
findings.evidence:
uint256 received = asset.balanceOf(address(this)) - beforeBalance;
if (received != amount) revert UnsupportedTokenBehavior();

findings.title: unchecked block in withdraw is safe but requires reader trust
findings.description: The subtraction is bounded by the currentBalance < amount check above. The unchecked block is correct but requires manual verification.
findings.impact: No overflow risk. Reduces auditability for readers unfamiliar with the pattern.
findings.recommendation: Add a NatSpec comment above the unchecked block explaining why underflow is impossible.

findings.title: No pause mechanism for emergency response
findings.description: There is no way to halt deposits/withdrawals if a vulnerability is discovered post-deployment.
findings.impact: Cannot pause contract in an emergency. Acceptable for hackathon scope; relevant for production.
findings.recommendation: For production, add OpenZeppelin Pausable with owner-controlled pause()/unpause().
findings.evidence: contract TokenVault is ReentrancyGuard { // no Pausable

positive_patterns: ReentrancyGuard applied to all state-changing functions
SafeERC20 used for all ERC-20 interactions
Checks-effects-interactions pattern correctly followed in withdraw
Custom errors used throughout — gas-efficient and readable
balanceOf is private mapping with public accessor
recoverForeignToken correctly blocks recovery of the primary asset token
asset and owner are immutable — reduces attack surface
agent_tasks: Verify SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom reverts correctly when transferFrom returns false
Test TC-001: deploy with test wallet, simulate wallet loss, verify recoverForeignToken fails
Test TC-002: attempt deposit with mock fee-on-transfer token — confirm UnsupportedTokenBehavior revert
Document the EOA owner limitation in FINAL_REPORT.md as a known production consideration
